component.name: Checkbox
component.description: A boolean toggle with a visible label. Wraps a native <input type='checkbox'> styled with design tokens. Use for independent on/off options; for exclusive choices use radio inputs. Supports checked, unchecked, disabled, and disabled-checked states.
component.figmaNodeId: none — no Figma component set exists; uses text styles only

when: The user can independently toggle a single option on or off
Multiple independent options can be enabled simultaneously (notification preferences, feature flags)
The user must confirm an agreement or consent

patterns.name: notification-preferences
patterns.description: A group of independent Checkboxes in a Stack, each representing a toggleable preference.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using Checkbox for mutually exclusive choices
antiPatterns.reason: Multiple checkboxes look like independent options; users expect to be able to select more than one.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use native radio inputs for single-select-from-set (e.g. gender, payment method).

antiPatterns.reason: The label is required for accessibility — the checkbox is not self-describing without visible text.
antiPatterns.alternative: Always pass a label. If the design places the label elsewhere, pass it as aria-label via ...rest.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using Checkbox as a substitute for Switch/Toggle
antiPatterns.reason: Checkboxes imply a saved state that requires a submit action; toggles imply immediate effect.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use native <input type='checkbox'> with an immediate onChange handler and role='switch' until a Switch component is added.

purpose.default: Single boolean toggle. Checked/disabled states are controlled via standard HTML props.

states.description: Stronger border on hover to signal interactivity.
Muted fill and border; checkmark still visible.
